# Port status matrix

Target: WRF 4.7.1 at `f52c197ed39d12e087d02c50f412d90d418f6186`.

Whole-model parity: **0% (Not started)**. A Rust executable consumes WRF initialization and matches an upstream integration trajectory.

| Area | State | Evidence | Next gate | Tracking |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Source pin and license | Complete | UPSTREAM.toml and scripts/fetch-wrf.sh pin and verify WRF v4.7.1. | Retarget only through an explicit issue and compatibility audit. | — |
| ESMF-derived time/calendar | Complete active surface | All 93 active Test1 cases and both Fortran interfaces match the golden output. | Add cases when later WRF callers expose untested behavior. | — |
| Registry and configuration | In progress | Typed dimspec, state, and rconfig parsing now includes nested source expansion, conditional definitions, physical source locations, and six exact generated-artifact goldens. | Packages, typedefs, communication entries, and remaining generators. | [#85](https://github.com/mikeortman/wrf-rs/issues/85) |
| Domain decomposition and halo exchange | In progress | Exact task_for_point.c decomposition, period.c parity, and deterministic local and four-rank MPI results. | Generated communication descriptors, multi-field aggregation, nesting, and broader process grids. | [#88](https://github.com/mikeortman/wrf-rs/issues/88) |
| ARW dynamical core | In progress | Dependency-closed dry, acoustic, and specified-boundary kernels have direct pinned-Fortran parity; the local acoustic trajectory matches 2,196 selected final values; the complete three-stage dry large-step boundary sequence (first-substep relaxation, tendency assembly, boundary assignment) is composed behind one failure-atomic capability. | Compose specified updates and halo exchange around the verified acoustic trajectory. | [#86](https://github.com/mikeortman/wrf-rs/issues/86) |
| Physics drivers and schemes | In progress | Kessler warm-rain microphysics matches all 660 mutable oracle values, and the typed microphysics driver (disabled/Kessler dispatch, Registry moisture-species order, specified/channel tile clipping, multi-call precipitation accumulation) matches 8,624 driver-branch oracle values across seven pinned cases with deterministic worker counts. | Registry-parsed moisture packages, then a coupled precipitation trajectory. | [#87](https://github.com/mikeortman/wrf-rs/issues/87) |
| I/O and NetCDF metadata | In progress | The minimum typed ARW schema writes independently comparable restart metadata and field bits. | Full Registry-selected state, alarms, NetCDF-4 policy, and resumed-trajectory parity. | [#84](https://github.com/mikeortman/wrf-rs/issues/84) |
| WRFDA, WRF-Chem, WRF-Hydro, and TL/adjoint | Not started | No accepted compatibility slice. | Create separate workstreams after the ARW baseline. | — |
| End-to-end idealized and regression suite | Not started | No Rust executable trajectory accepted yet. | em_b_wave, then em_squall2d_x differential runs. | [#8](https://github.com/mikeortman/wrf-rs/issues/8) |

This matrix describes accepted interfaces and evidence, not a percentage extrapolated from file counts.
Mutable work state and sequencing live in GitHub Issues and the WRF Rust Port Project.
